description: 01.AI's flagship Yi Large model. ~$3.00/$9.00 per 1M tokens via hosting providers.
Most powerful Jamba long-context model. $2.00/$8.00 per 1M tokens.
Efficient, lightweight Jamba model. $0.20/$0.40 per 1M tokens.
Qwen reasoning model (QwQ). $0.80/$2.40 per 1M tokens.
Fast Qwen model. $0.115/$0.46 per 1M tokens, 1M context.
Balanced Qwen model. ~$0.40/$1.20 per 1M tokens.
Cheapest Qwen text tier. ~$0.05/$0.20 per 1M tokens.
Alibaba's flagship Qwen model. $1.20/$4.80 per 1M tokens (≤128K context).
Amazon's fast, low-cost Nova tier on Bedrock — multimodal and cost-effective for high-volume everyday tasks, with a 300K context. $0.06/$0.24 per 1M tokens.
Smallest, cheapest Nova model. $0.035/$0.14 per 1M tokens. Text only.

Amazon's most capable Nova model on Bedrock — built for complex tasks needing deep context, multistep planning, and tool coordination; multimodal (text/image/video) with a 1M-token context, and a teacher model for distillation. $2.50/$12.50 per 1M tokens.
Amazon's balanced Nova tier on Bedrock — a capable multimodal model tuned for accuracy, speed, and cost across a wide range of tasks, with a 300K context. $0.80/$3.20 per 1M tokens.

Anthropic's flagship Mythos-class model for ambitious, long-running work — strong at coding, knowledge work, vision, and computer use, with a 1M-token context window and up to 128K output tokens. $10/$50 per 1M tokens (90% prompt-caching discount on input).
Retired on Anthropic API (still available via Bedrock/Vertex). $0.80/$4 per 1M tokens.
Anthropic's fast, low-cost tier — ~97 tokens/sec, built for high-volume classification, routing, and code review where heavy reasoning isn't needed. 200K context. $1/$5 per 1M tokens.
Deprecated. Previously the top Opus model at $15/$75 per 1M tokens. Use Opus 4.8 instead.
Opus-tier model with hybrid reasoning. $5/$25 per 1M tokens, 200K context.

Anthropic's Opus 4.6 — a frontier model with class-leading coding (~80.8% SWE-bench Verified) and standout legal/enterprise reasoning (top BigLaw Bench score). 200K context, prompt caching up to 90% off. $5/$25 per 1M tokens.

Anthropic's most capable generally available Opus, tuned for advanced software engineering, long-horizon autonomy, instruction-following, and high-resolution multimodal understanding. 200K context. $5/$25 per 1M tokens.

Anthropic's Opus flagship, refined over 4.7 with broad benchmark gains and a 'dynamic workflows' tool for large multi-step tasks, plus effort control and a faster, cheaper fast mode. $5/$25 per 1M tokens.
Sonnet-tier model with hybrid reasoning. $3/$15 per 1M tokens.
Latest Sonnet model. Strong balance of intelligence and speed at $3/$15 per 1M tokens.

price_note: Launch/intro pricing: $2/$10 per 1M tokens through Aug 31 2026; reverts to standard $3/$15 on Sep 1 2026.
description: Anthropic's most agentic Sonnet yet — performance approaching Opus 4.8 at a fraction of the price, strong on reasoning, tool use, coding and autonomous agent workloads. Launch pricing is $2/$10 per 1M tokens through Aug 31 2026; reverts to standard $3/$15 on Sep 1 2026.
Baichuan M2 32B model. $0.07/$0.07 per 1M tokens via hosting providers.
Cohere's flagship model. $1/$2 per 1M tokens — far cheaper than the older Command R+.
Earlier Command R version. $0.50/$1.50 per 1M tokens.
Smaller Command model. $0.15/$0.60 per 1M tokens.
Previous-generation flagship. $2.50/$10 per 1M tokens. Command A is the newer, cheaper successor.
Multimodal embedding model. $0.12 per 1M tokens (text and image).
Reranking model for search and retrieval. $0.02 per 1M tokens.
DeepSeek's fast, ultra-cheap open-weight (MIT) MoE — 284B parameters (13B active) with a native 1M-token context and aggressive prompt caching. $0.14/$0.28 per 1M tokens.

DeepSeek's open-weight (MIT) flagship — a 1.6T-parameter MoE (49B active) with a 1M-token context and thinking/non-thinking modes. Leads open models on world knowledge, math, and coding (~80.6% SWE-bench Verified, top open-weights). $0.44/$0.87 per 1M tokens.
DeepSeek V4 Flash on Fireworks. $0.14/$0.28 per 1M; cached $0.028.

Qwen 3.7 Plus on Fireworks. $0.40/$1.60 per 1M; cached $0.08.
One of the cheapest hosted APIs. $0.075/$0.30 per 1M tokens, fast and multimodal.
Lightweight, low-cost model. $0.10/$0.40 per 1M tokens.
Still available. 2M context window, strong reasoning. $1.25/$10 per 1M tokens.
Fast, balanced model. $0.30/$2.50 per 1M tokens, 1M context.
Ultra-cheap, fast model. $0.10/$0.40 per 1M tokens with full multimodal support.
Frontier reasoning model. $2/$12 per 1M (≤200K context), 2M context window.

Google's fast, low-cost workhorse from I/O 2026 — strong multimodal reasoning and coding (beats Gemini 3.1 Pro), with native computer-use for agents. The default Gemini across Search and the API. $1.50/$9 per 1M tokens.
Google's text embedding model. $0.025 per 1M tokens.
OpenAI's open-source 120B model on Groq. ~500 tokens/sec. $0.15/$0.60 per 1M.
OpenAI's open-source 20B model on Groq's LPU. ~1000 tokens/sec. $0.075/$0.30 per 1M.
Llama 3.1 8B on Groq's LPU. ~840 tokens/sec — extremely fast. $0.05/$0.08 per 1M tokens.
Llama 3.3 70B on Groq's LPU. ~394 tokens/sec. $0.59/$0.79 per 1M tokens.
Meta's Llama 4 Scout served on Groq's LPU for high-speed inference — a natively multimodal MoE (17B active, 16 experts) with a 10M-token context. $0.11/$1.00 per 1M tokens.
Qwen 3.6 27B on Groq's LPU. ~500 tokens/sec. $0.60/$3.00 per 1M tokens.
Qwen3 32B on Groq's LPU. ~662 tokens/sec. $0.29/$0.59 per 1M tokens.

Small, fast open-weights model. Cheapest hosted price via Groq: $0.05/$0.08 per 1M tokens.
Widely used open-weights model. Cheapest hosted price via Groq: $0.59/$0.79 per 1M tokens (also on Together at $1.04/$1.04).

Meta's open-weight Llama 4 Scout — a natively multimodal MoE (17B active / 109B total, 16 experts) with an industry-leading 10M-token context, the longest of any open-weight model. Handles text, images, and video; fits on a single H100 with Int4. $0.11/$0.34 per 1M tokens.
MiniMax M2 base model. $0.30/$1.20 per 1M; cached $0.03.
MiniMax M2.1 model. $0.30/$1.20 per 1M; cached $0.03.
MiniMax M2.5 model. $0.30/$1.20 per 1M; cached $0.03.
MiniMax M2.7 model. $0.30/$1.20 per 1M; cached $0.06. 205K context.
MiniMax's open-weight flagship built on its MSA sparse-attention architecture, combining frontier-level coding, a 1M-token context, and native multimodality. $0.30/$1.20 per 1M tokens.
Code generation model. $0.30/$0.90 per 1M tokens, 256K context.
Code generation model. $0.30/$0.90 per 1M tokens, 256K context.
Mistral's 123B-parameter agentic coding model, built for autonomous software-engineering workflows with a 256K context. $0.40/$2.00 per 1M tokens.
Mistral's enterprise reasoning model — transparent multi-step chain-of-thought with multilingual reasoning across 8+ languages; Mistral's answer to o3/o4-mini-class reasoners. $2/$5 per 1M tokens.
Smallest Mistral model for simple tasks. $0.10/$0.10 per 1M tokens.

Mistral's largest open-weight model — a 675B-parameter MoE (41B active) under Apache 2.0, strong on knowledge and math (93.6% MATH-500), though not a dedicated reasoning model. $0.50/$1.50 per 1M tokens.
Affordable medium-tier model. $0.40/$2.00 per 1M tokens.
Mistral's agentic mid-tier — ~77.6% SWE-bench Verified with strong tool use, now Mistral's recommended coding model. $1.50/$7.50 per 1M tokens.

Earlier Kimi model. $0.60/$3.00 per 1M; cached $0.10.
Kimi's latest multimodal model. $0.95/$4.00 per 1M; cached $0.16.

Moonshot's coding-focused 1T-parameter MoE model with a 256K context and forced 'thinking' mode, tuned for agentic coding with ~30% lower reasoning-token use than K2.6. OpenAI-compatible API, Modified-MIT weights. $0.95/$4.00 per 1M tokens.
High-speed Kimi K2.7 Code. $1.90/$8.00 per 1M; cached $0.38. ~180 TPS.
Large NVIDIA-tuned Llama model. $0.60/$3.60 per 1M on hosted platforms.
NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Ultra reasoning model. $0.60/$3.60 per 1M on hosted platforms.
NVIDIA Nemotron 70B Instruct. ~$0.10/$0.10 per 1M on DeepInfra; free NIM tier.
Still available. 1M context window, strong at coding and instruction following.
Compact, affordable model with 1M context. Good for high-throughput tasks.
Smallest and cheapest GPT-4.1 variant. Ideal for classification and simple tasks at scale.
High-capability model with 1M context. Balanced price-to-performance for most professional tasks.
Smaller, faster, cheaper variant of GPT-5.4. Good for high-volume workloads.
Frontier model for coding and professional work. 1.05M context window with multimodal input.
Image generation model. $8 input / $30 output per 1M tokens.
Realtime voice and audio model. Text tokens $4/$24 per 1M; audio tokens $32/$64 per 1M.
Reasoning model with extended thinking. Still available for cost-conscious reasoning workloads.
Reasoning model with extended thinking. Cost-effective for complex problem solving.
OpenAI's large embedding model. $0.13 per 1M tokens.
OpenAI's small embedding model. $0.02 per 1M tokens.
Highest-capability GPT-5.5 tier for the hardest professional and research workloads. 1.05M context, multimodal input.
Highest-capability GPT-5.4 tier for demanding reasoning and professional work. 1.05M context, multimodal input.
Smallest, cheapest GPT-5.4 variant. Built for classification and high-volume tasks at scale.
Chat-tuned GPT-5.3 for conversational and general-purpose use. 128K context, multimodal input.
GPT-5.3 optimized for agentic coding and software engineering. 400K context.
Perplexity's lightweight web-grounded model — real-time web search with cited, conversational answers for quick factual lookups. $1/$1 per 1M tokens plus per-request search fees.
Deep research model. $2/$8 per 1M, citations $2/1M, search $5/1K, reasoning $3/1M.
Perplexity's web-grounded model for in-depth, multi-step queries — retrieves and synthesizes multiple web searches with ~2x the citations of standard Sonar. $3/$15 per 1M tokens plus search fees.
Perplexity's web-grounded reasoning model — chain-of-thought over real-time web search with inline citations, for analytical, multi-source questions. $2/$8 per 1M tokens plus per-request search fees.
Superior multimodal model for complex tasks. $2.00/$6.00 per 1M tokens.
Compact multimodal model for on-device execution. $0.10/$0.10 per 1M.

GLM-4.5 model. $0.60/$2.20 per 1M; cached input $0.11.

Z.AI's GLM-4.6 — strong tool use and search-agent performance with tighter agent-framework integration, ~30% more token-efficient than GLM-4.5. 200K context. $0.60/$2.20 per 1M tokens.
Vision model (GLM-4.6V). $0.30/$0.90 per 1M; cached $0.05.

Z.AI's GLM-4.7 — a development-focused open model with gains in agentic coding and multi-step reasoning (~73.8% SWE-bench) and stable 'think-then-act' execution in Claude Code, Cline, and similar agents. $0.60/$2.20 per 1M tokens.

price_note: Limited-time free tier ($0/$0). Z.AI has not published an end date — verify current pricing before relying on $0.
description: Free GLM flash model from Z.AI — $0/$0 per 1M tokens (genuinely free, not a data error). Limited-time free tier.

Z.AI's flagship open-weights model — a 744B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts (~40B active per token) with a 1M-token context and MIT-licensed weights. Tops open-weight leaderboards and rivals frontier models on long-horizon coding. ~$1.40/$4.40 per 1M tokens via hosts.

Anthropic Claude Mythos 5. Limited availability (Glasswing program). $10/$50 per 1M tokens.
availability_note: Reportedly suspended in June 2026 under a US export-control directive — availability unconfirmed. Verify with Anthropic before relying on it.
